title: RSA key size 2048 bits — should document minimum or upgrade path
description: In persona.rs:11, RSA keys are generated at 2048 bits. NIST SP 800-131A Rev 2 and BSI TR-02102-1 both recommend 3072-bit RSA as the new minimum for keys protecting data beyond 2030. For an ActivityPub server where private keys are long-lived (they persist in the DB for the lifetime of the persona and cannot be rotated without breaking existing follower relationships), generating 2048-bit keys today creates a migration problem in ~4 years. Mastodon itself recently defaulted to 4096 for new accounts. The current choice is defensible for interop but should be documented as a known limitation. Fix: change the default to 3072 or 4096 bits; note that keygen takes a few hundred ms at 4096 which is acceptable for a one-time CLI operation. At minimum add a comment explaining the choice.
close_reason: P2-P3: RateLimiter::new returns Self, remaining items accepted as known tech debt or tracked in hardening epic
